failures.issue: The course home hid readiness, program position, next-course routes, and evidence boundaries.
failures.remedy: Expose recommended preparation, downstream routes, reviewed date, and deterministic-versus-external evidence labels.

failures.issue: The order argument and query/key roles were incomplete and causally ambiguous.
failures.remedy: Define query, key, and value before use and trace a token permutation through all three position designs.

failures.issue: The scaled dot-product trace used an incorrect division convention.
failures.remedy: Use division by the square root of key width consistently and recompute the numerical attention trace.

failures.issue: The 3-bit range and reconstruction example were asymmetric and numerically misleading.
failures.remedy: Use a declared symmetric level convention, show rounding errors, and separate footprint arithmetic from quality evidence.

failures.issue: Request-rate and concurrency quantities mixed incompatible units.
failures.remedy: Attach units to arrival rate, service time, tokens, and goodput and recompute the capacity example.

failures.issue: A deterministic fixture was described too much like measured evaluation evidence.
failures.remedy: Label the fixture explicitly and distinguish arithmetic checks from benchmark, human, or model-grader measurements.

failures.issue: The adapter initialization left both factors unable to receive the intended first gradient.
failures.remedy: Use one random and one zero factor, trace the first backward pass, and assert the expected gradient ownership.

failures.issue: The reference implied edit efficacy that its deterministic rows had not measured.
failures.remedy: Separate observation, intervention, efficacy, locality, and side-effect evidence and bound the artifact claim.

failures.issue: The course home did not expose readiness, prior-course reuse, later routes, or evidence boundaries.
failures.remedy: Add explicit preparation, continuation, reviewed date, milestone, and fixture-versus-external evidence guidance.

failures.issue: Node and edge target timing conventions disagreed across explanation and interaction.
failures.remedy: Choose one explicit state-transition convention and align observation, reward, continuation, code, and assessment.

failures.issue: The home lacked program position, prerequisite activation, continuation routes, and a clear evidence boundary.
failures.remedy: Expose readiness, prior and next courses, build milestones, reviewed date, and optional external-run status.

failures.issue: Cross-entropy was named without a complete event-space and numerical target trace.
failures.remedy: Derive empirical cross-entropy with probabilities, units, masks, and a one-hot changed case.

failures.issue: The causal factorization did not prove prefix invariance at every prediction position.
failures.remedy: Trace the product factorization and edit a future token while asserting all earlier conditional outputs remain unchanged.

failures.issue: The MCMC discussion omitted invariant kernels and the MALA proposal correction.
failures.remedy: Teach stationarity, detailed balance limits, and a full forward/reverse MALA acceptance calculation.

failures.issue: The denoising score target and loss were not explicitly derived from corrupted data.
failures.remedy: Show corruption, conditional score target, prediction, squared loss, and the marginal-score evidence boundary.

failures.issue: Reverse sampling was described without a complete numerical update and primitive model-call accounting.
failures.remedy: Work one scalar reverse step and compare samplers using paired starts and exact network evaluations.

failures.issue: Memory, throughput, resume, and provenance failures were collapsed into a generic systems summary.
failures.remedy: Provide separate byte, rate, next-step-state, and immutable-data ledgers with diagnostic retries.

failures.issue: The downloadable matrix covered fewer seed-by-sample-count cells than the page promised.
failures.remedy: Complete all 20-seed by two-count canonical cells and label unexecuted entries as planned nulls.

failures.issue: The declared seed-by-treatment reference matrix was incomplete or insufficiently status-labeled.
failures.remedy: Preserve every canonical seed-treatment cell with explicit fixture, planned, or measured provenance.

failures.issue: The reference covered fewer paired starting noises than the 32-start sampler study promised.
failures.remedy: Provide all 32-start by two-sampler cells with planned-null status until authentic execution exists.

failures.issue: The condition-by-scale-by-seed matrix did not contain every promised comparison cell.
failures.remedy: Complete the full canonical Cartesian matrix and retain conflict and hard-gate decision cases.

failures.issue: The RL home hid preparation, continuation, reviewed provenance, and required-versus-optional evidence status.
failures.remedy: Expose readiness, prior-course reuse, Embodied AI continuation, milestones, and exact evidence boundaries.

failures.issue: The discounting example did not clearly show how gamma changes a delayed reward.
failures.remedy: Compute the same reward sequence at declared gamma values and explain the continuing-task boundary.

failures.issue: Belief-update arithmetic and transition-before-observation timing disagreed across surfaces.
failures.remedy: Use one normalized Bayesian update with a declared transition and reproduce it in explanation, lab, and code.

failures.issue: The displayed policy-evaluation backup value was arithmetically inconsistent.
failures.remedy: Recompute the exact backup and align the numerical result across the lesson and interaction.

failures.issue: The policy-ratio example mixed probabilities that could not produce the shown ratio.
failures.remedy: Use a coherent old/new categorical policy and recompute ratio, clipping, and advantage contribution.

failures.issue: The downloadable seed-by-arm evidence matrix omitted declared rows.
failures.remedy: Complete the canonical five-seed matrix, attach per-row transition budgets, and retain constraint failures.

failures.issue: The page promised five paired seeds while the reference contained only three.
failures.remedy: Join the promise to a five-seed source contract and include primitive budgets on all ten rows.

failures.issue: The DQN artifact omitted promised seeds and did not make interaction/update budgets recomputable per row.
failures.remedy: Supply all five paired seeds with environment-interaction and optimizer-update budgets and failure gates.

failures.issue: The actor-critic artifact omitted promised seeds and policy-update budget units.
failures.remedy: Supply all five paired seeds with interactions, epochs, and the retained KL or finite-value failure.

failures.issue: The matched planner artifact was incomplete and did not expose real-step versus model-call budgets.
failures.remedy: Complete five seed-by-horizon rows and attach both real-environment and model-call units.

failures.issue: The action-target serialization leaked or ambiguously exposed future actions and its matrix was incomplete.
failures.remedy: Shift action targets to pre-action prefixes, add a future-edit invariant, and complete canonical paired rows.

failures.issue: The lesson smoke/full numbers and seed protocol drifted from the actual DQN runner.
failures.remedy: Align the lesson, contract, runner, runbook, stop rules, and expected invariant fields exactly.

failures.issue: The final seed-by-intervention artifact omitted rows and did not preserve primitive budgets.
failures.remedy: Provide all five paired seed rows, explicit budget units, mixed outcomes, failures, and a bounded decision.

failures.issue: The home lacked explicit prerequisite reuse, final program position, and evidence boundaries.
failures.remedy: Expose four recommended prior courses, final-course role, milestones, reviewed date, and simulation limits.

failures.issue: The trace moved an object without declaring a physical grasp attachment.
failures.remedy: Model grasp validity explicitly and contrast attached, ungrasped, and stale-state consequences.

failures.issue: The causal action serialization exposed the action target in its own input row.
failures.remedy: Place each action prediction on a pre-action observation row and verify a future-action edit cannot alter its prefix.

failures.issue: Success-rate uncertainty ignored the independent episode unit.
failures.remedy: Report cell counts and Wilson intervals at the episode level and keep assistance and constraints non-compensable.

failures.issue: The latency lesson called p50 an average and did not enforce the p99 SLO correctly.
failures.remedy: Name p50 as the median, compare p99 with the 50 ms deadline, and preserve rollback authority.

failures.issue: The project lacked a runnable end-to-end starter and sufficiently inspectable failure evidence.
failures.remedy: Provide a dependency-free task starter with replay, requested/applied action, stale-packet, and boundary checks.

failures.issue: The project lacked a runnable estimator starter and explicit delayed-sensor abstention evidence.
failures.remedy: Provide a synchronized update fixture, stale-packet branch, truth-separation check, and reusable dossier schema.

failures.issue: The project did not execute a complete support-departure and intervention starter trace.
failures.remedy: Provide a closed-loop support fixture that preserves first departure, applied fallback, and assisted status.

failures.issue: The project lacked executable target-shift, grounding, latency, and hard-gate checks.
failures.remedy: Provide a runnable causal-prefix fixture and make grounding and deadline failures block policy ranking.

failures.issue: The project lacked a runnable detect-recover-resume authority handoff.
failures.remedy: Execute a fault-injection starter that records requested/applied action, fallback ownership, and assisted resume.

failures.issue: The final project lacked a complete paired starter matrix and non-compensable deadline decision.
failures.remedy: Provide the full seed-by-arm starter, matched budgets, mixed outcome, retained deadline failure, and evidence boundary.
